objections.claim: The proposal misstates the schema evidence. In `tests/schema.test.js`, `embedding` is optional: `validateExperience()` only errors when `embedding` is present and neither `null` nor an array. So the proposal's statement that the canonical schema requires `embedding` and that this record is missing every required field except `timestamp` and `salience` is false.
objections.falsifiable_test: Read `validateExperience()` in `tests/schema.test.js` or run it on an experience object that omits `embedding`. If no embedding-related error is produced, this objection holds; if omission fails validation, it is false.

objections.claim: The proposal's origin story is inconsistent with current code. `applyDrResults()` in `userspace.js` filters `kv_operations` to `pattern:`, `desire:`, `tactic:`, `principle:`, `config:`, and `prompt:` keys only, so the current DR apply path cannot write `experience:` records at all. Blaming the present malformed key on DR's normal apply path is therefore not supported by the code.
objections.falsifiable_test: Call `applyDrResults()` with an output that contains `kv_operations: [{ key: 'experience:test', op: 'put', value: {...} }]`. If `K.kvWriteGated()` is invoked for that experience key, this objection is false; if the op is filtered out, it holds.

objections.claim: The proposal misidentifies the affected subsystem. `eval.js` never reads stored `experience:` records; it evaluates the current action from the live ledger, desires, and patterns only. Historical experiences are consumed by reflection in `reflect.js` via `selectExperiences()`, so saying 'the eval pipeline will struggle to use it' is false.
objections.falsifiable_test: Search `eval.js` for any load or read of `experience:` keys, then compare with `reflect.js`, which calls `K.kvList({ prefix: 'experience:' })` and `selectExperiences()`. If `eval.js` does consume stored experiences, this objection is false; if only reflect does, it holds.

objections.claim: Deleting the record is not risk-free because the malformed experience is not inert today. `gatherReflectContext()` loads every `experience:` entry, and `selectExperiences()` only requires `timestamp` and numeric `salience` to score a record. This malformed record therefore remains eligible for deep-reflect selection even without `observation`, `desire_alignment`, `pattern_delta`, or `embedding`.
objections.falsifiable_test: Pass the malformed record to `selectExperiences()` with a valid `timestamp` and `salience`. If it is filtered out solely because it lacks canonical fields, this objection is false; if it is scored and returned, it holds.

objections.claim: The proposal's redundancy claim about `experience:1775569549222` is false under current retrieval semantics. In the captured snapshot, `experience:session_probe_wake_no_action_example` is newer (`2026-04-07T13:45:56.280Z`) and higher-salience (`0.7`) than `experience:1775569549222` (`2026-04-07T13:45:49.139Z`, `0.6`), and `selectExperiences()` sorts by salience-based score. Deleting the malformed record therefore changes which memory deep-reflect surfaces first, even if the prose is similar.
objections.falsifiable_test: Run `selectExperiences()` on just these two snapshot records. If `experience:1775569549222` still ranks ahead of the malformed record, this objection is false; if the malformed record ranks first because of its timestamp/salience, it holds.

objections.claim: If this proposal is implemented through the existing DR proposal/apply mechanism, it will not actually perform the stated fix. `applyDrResults()` drops `experience:` operations before calling `kvWriteGated()`, so an in-system proposal that tries to delete `experience:session_probe_wake_no_action_example` will be a no-op unless code changes are made first.
objections.falsifiable_test: Feed `applyDrResults()` an output containing `{ key: 'experience:session_probe_wake_no_action_example', op: 'delete' }`. If the delete reaches `K.kvWriteGated()` and removes the key, this objection is false; if it is ignored by the filter, it holds.
